Errors.localStorageDisabled_description: It seems that for one reason or another, your browser's local storage is not accessible for the App. Characters of the Mist relies solely on browser local storage for data management and persistance, and does not use a server at all. This means that in your case, it cannot run.

CharacterSheetPage.dropToImport: Drop a file here to import it. A board opens as a new board, a full character replaces your active one, and a card or tracker imports into the loaded character.
CharacterSheetPage.cannotDropWrongGame: Can't drop here, this component is for a different game system.

SidebarMenu.updateCharacterConfirmDescription: This character's contents will be replaced by the file's, keeping its place in your drawer and any board links pointing at it. The drawer copy updates only when you Save. This can't be undone.
SidebarMenu.updateBoardConfirmDescription: This board's contents will be replaced by the file's, keeping its place in your drawer. The drawer copy updates only when you Save. This can't be undone.
SidebarMenu.resetConfirmTitle: Are you sure you want to reset your character?
SidebarMenu.resetConfirmDescription: This action is destructive. Your active character will be deleted and replaced with a blank character. If you don't reload the app, you can still recover your old character with the undo functionality.
SidebarMenu.clearBoardConfirmTitle: Are you sure you want to clear the board?
SidebarMenu.clearBoardConfirmDescription: This removes every item from the board. The board's name, position, and grid are kept, and you can bring the items back with the undo functionality (as long as you don't reload the app).
SidebarMenu.unloadConfirmTitle: Are you sure you want to return to the menu?
SidebarMenu.unloadConfirmDescription: This action is extremely destructive. Your active character will be unloaded and the history erased. You will not be able to undo this, make sure you either saved your character to the drawer or exported it.

seeds.title: Generate from seeds (optional)
seeds.primaryInfo: Your main action color, for buttons and highlights.
seeds.backgroundInfo: The hue your surfaces are tinted with.
seeds.accentInfo: A second highlight color, distinct from the primary.
seeds.secondaryInfo: A quieter color for secondary buttons and surfaces.
seeds.saturationMinimalDesc: Near-grey surfaces; the calmest, most neutral look.
seeds.saturationBalancedDesc: A moderate tint; colorful but easy on the eyes.
seeds.saturationVividDesc: Boldly tinted surfaces and a strong accent.
seeds.contrastNormalDesc: The standard spread, like the built-in presets.
seeds.contrastContrastedDesc: A wider spread and crisper borders for more punch.
seeds.confirmDescription: This replaces all of the theme's colors with ones derived from the seeds. Your roundness is kept, and you can keep editing afterwards.

tokenPurpose.background: The app's main canvas color. The backdrop behind everything.
tokenPurpose.foreground: Default text and icons on the app canvas.
tokenPurpose.card: Raised panels that sit on the canvas, like dialogs, cards and sheets.
tokenPurpose.card-foreground: Text on raised panels and cards.
tokenPurpose.popover: Floating surfaces that pop over the page, like menus, dropdowns and tooltips.
tokenPurpose.popover-foreground: Text inside floating menus and popovers.
tokenPurpose.primary: Your main action color, used for primary buttons and key highlights.
tokenPurpose.primary-foreground: Text and icons on the primary action color.
tokenPurpose.ring: The focus outline around whatever control you've tabbed onto.
tokenPurpose.secondary: Secondary surfaces and buttons, the quieter alternative to the primary action.
tokenPurpose.secondary-foreground: Text on secondary surfaces and buttons.
tokenPurpose.muted: Subtle, inactive surfaces, like disabled fields, skeletons and gentle backdrops.
tokenPurpose.muted-foreground: Secondary, de-emphasized text, like captions, hints and placeholders.
tokenPurpose.accent: The hover and highlight surface. What a row or button turns when you point at it.
tokenPurpose.accent-foreground: Text on a hovered or highlighted surface.
tokenPurpose.destructive: Dangerous, irreversible actions, like delete, remove and reset.
tokenPurpose.destructive-foreground: Text and icons on the destructive color.
tokenPurpose.border: Dividers and outlines between sections and around panels.
tokenPurpose.input: The outline around text fields and inputs.

paper.title: Paper
paper.intro: The look of game-agnostic paper elements like drawer trackers. The same in light and dark.

tokenPurpose.paper-background: The parchment behind a paper element, like a drawer tracker's body.
tokenPurpose.paper-foreground: The ink: default text on the paper.
tokenPurpose.paper-primary: The header bar across the top of a paper element.
tokenPurpose.paper-primary-foreground: Text and icons on the header bar.
tokenPurpose.paper-secondary: Quieter chips and surfaces on the paper, like tags.
tokenPurpose.paper-border: The outline around a paper element and its dividers.
tokenPurpose.paper-accent: The standout accent on paper, like a highlighted edge.
tokenPurpose.paper-destructive: Dangerous bits on paper, like a burn or remove.

InfoDialog.title: About
InfoDialog.description: Information, credits, and licensing for this application.

content.about:
#### What is Characters of the Mist?

Characters of the Mist is an **unofficial**, fan-made character creator and manager for the TTRPGs from [Amit Moshe and Son of Oak Game Studio LLC](https://cityofmist.co). It's built from the ground up with a modern and responsive design, and all your data is saved directly in your browser for complete privacy and control. This tool is in no way endorsed by Amit Moshe or Son of Oak Game Studio LLC.

---

#### What can this tool do?

This tool is meant to help you create and update your character sheet quickly and easily. It allows you to edit your Theme Cards, Statuses, and Name, and save everything automatically. Beyond the basics, it's packed with powerful features to make managing your character a breeze:

* A versatile **Drawer** that acts like a complete file system for your character components, with full drag-and-drop organization and a compact view for managing many items.

* Intuitive **drag-and-drop** for almost everything: reorder your cards and trackers, move items between folders, or even load an entire character by dragging it from the drawer.

* A fully **dynamic character sheet** with flexible cards. You can even switch to a **side-by-side view** if you prefer to see the front and back of cards at the same time.

* A powerful **Undo & Redo system** (`Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y`) that will allow you to fix your mistakes whether they happened in the Drawer or on your Character Sheet.

* A **Command Palette** (`Ctrl+K` or `Cmd+K`) that lets you find and use any feature in the app without touching your mouse.

* **Game-related personalization** with multiple color themes assorted to Mist TTRPGs and a light/dark mode toggle to make the app feel like your own.

---

#### Can this tool be used as a VTT?

As it is right now, this tool is only a character manager. It doesn't support battlemaps, handouts, dice rollers or further automatization. You are free to fork this project and add those features, as long as the license terms are respected.

---

#### Can I report a problem with the app?

You can send me a message on [the official Tools of the Mist discord](https://discord.gg/c7GBPyTcWW) should you encounter a problem with the app, and I will see what I can do about it. Keep in mind however that this app is distributed AS-IS, with no guarantees whatsoever.
content.license:
#### What license is this code under?

This app is under the [GNU Affero General Public License v3.0](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0.html).

This means you are free to use, copy, modify, and redistribute the code, including for commercial purposes. However, if you distribute or run a modified version (including hosting it for others over a network), you must make your full source code available under this same license.
content.credits:
#### Credits & Attributions

Thank you to [Son of Oak Game Studio LLC](https://cityofmist.co) for creating City of Mist, Metro:OTHERSCAPE and Legend in the Mist. The City of Mist Logos and Mythos icons, as well as the :Otherscape Noise, Self and Mythos icons are the intellectual property of Son of Oak Game Studios. I do not claim any ownership on these icons, and will remove them from the app should it be requested.
content.localization:
#### Localization

Thank you to everyone who helped make Characters of the Mist available in languages other than English!

PatchNotesDialog.title: Patch Notes
PatchNotesDialog.description: You will see the patch notes for the latest versions in this window.

MigrationDialog.title: Migrate legacy character data
MigrationDialog.description: Here, you can migrate legacy character files from the alpha version of Characters of the Mist to the new, polished version. The Migrator will place the updated files at the root of your Drawer.
MigrationDialog.thankYou: If you are here, you most likely used the alpha version of Characters of the Mist. I would like to personally thank you for using my app, despite its many flaws. Much love,

LegacyDataDialog.title: Legacy data found!
LegacyDataDialog.info: Legacy data from the old, Alpha version of Characters of the Mist was found in your browser storage.
LegacyDataDialog.description: The App is going to delete this data as a cleanup measure. Before that, you can download your old data to use with the migration tools if you want.
LegacyDataDialog.warning: If you press Dismiss, the data will be deleted and lost forever. You cannot undo this. Pressing Download will trigger a browser download of the legacy data.
LegacyDataDialog.confirmationLabel: If you wish to dismiss your data, write the following confirmation text in the field below:

MainMenu.title: Characters of the Mist
MainMenu.subtitle: Welcome to Characters of the Mist, please pick a game system, or load a character directly.
MainMenu.hint: Pick a character sheet or a board above to get started

WelcomeDialog.title: Welcome to Characters of the Mist!
WelcomeDialog.description: This is an unofficial, fan-made character manager for TTRPGs by Son of Oak Game Studios. All your data is saved in your browser, ensuring complete privacy.
WelcomeDialog.feature1: A dynamic character sheet with full drag & drop functionality.
WelcomeDialog.feature2: A powerful Command Palette (Ctrl+K) for keyboard-only quick commands.
WelcomeDialog.feature3: A full file system in the Drawer to organize your characters and components.
WelcomeDialog.feature4: A robust Undo/Redo system to fix any mistakes.
WelcomeDialog.callToAction: There is a simple beginner's tutorial at your disposal, which you may start with the button below. Alternatively, you could go and read the patch notes. If you want to get straight to the app, you can simply close this window.
WelcomeDialog.needHelp: If you need help, or find a bug, you can reach out to the Tools of the Mist discord for support. You will find a link in the "About" section of the App Info window. Thank you for using Characters of the Mist, have fun!

CommandPalette.placeholder_3: Summon the Will of the Gods...
CommandPalette.placeholder_9: You don't need to hear this, you're a highly trained professional...
CommandPalette.placeholder_11: Let me guess, somebody stole your sweetroll...
CommandPalette.placeholder_15: I was crazy once, they put me in a room, a rubber room...
CommandPalette.placeholder_16: Wake up. This is a dream...
CommandPalette.placeholder_17: You again? Do you have no mouse? Oh well...
CommandPalette.placeholder_18: Something's wrong, I can feel it...
CommandPalette.placeholder_19: The Phantom Thieves are going to steal your heart...
CommandPalette.placeholder_20: I hope you paid Tom Nook back...
CommandPalette.placeholder_21: The Sun is a deadly laser...
CommandPalette.placeholder_24: May Zeus himself strike me down if I'm ly-...
CommandPalette.placeholder_25: This has got to be the worst copper I've ever seen...

CommandPalette.label: Command Palette

Tutorial.welcome_title: Welcome to Characters of the Mist!
Tutorial.welcome_content: This short tour will guide you through the main features. You can exit at any time by pressing the 'X' at the top right of this small window, or anywhere outside. If you exit by accident, you can start the tutorial again from the Settings menu.
Tutorial.mainMenuGames_content: Characters of the Mist supports three game systems: Legend in the Mist, City of Mist, and Otherscape. You can create characters for any of these systems.
Tutorial.mainMenuLegends_content: For this tutorial, we'll create a Legend in the Mist character. Click this card to select it, then we'll proceed to create your first character!
Tutorial.mainMenuCreate_content: Click this button to create a new Legend in the Mist character sheet. This will take you to the main character sheet interface.
Tutorial.sidebar_content: This is the main sidebar. It contains global actions like toggling edit mode, opening the file drawer, and accessing settings.
Tutorial.tabStrip_content: The strip at the top holds every character you have open. Click a tab to switch, drag tabs to reorder them, and press + to open another. You can also drag a character from the drawer onto the strip to open it, or drag a tab into the drawer to save it.
Tutorial.playArea_content: This is what Characters of the Mist can refer to as your "Play Area" or "Character Sheet". It holds your Trackers and Cards. They are all draggable, which lets you reorder them, or store them in the Drawer. More on that later. You can drop an external file here to import it.
Tutorial.characterName_content: You can edit your character's name here at any time. Your changes are saved automatically.
Tutorial.trackers_content: This area holds your Status and Story Tag trackers, along with your Story Theme trackers. You can add new ones when in 'Edit Mode'. Each Tracker has a sliding handle that appears upon hovering over them with your mouse. Using it, you can move them around or export them.
Tutorial.cards_content: This area holds your character's Cards, whether it is a Theme Card, or a Character Card. Each Card, like the Trackers, has a sliding handle that appears upon hovering over them. You will find a dragging grip to move them around and a few buttons to flip, delete (in Edit mode) or export the Card.
Tutorial.menuCollapse_content: This button lets you collapse the Sidebar into a much thinner version, with only icons on the buttons.
Tutorial.menuUndoRedo_content: These buttons let you Undo and Redo actions on your Character Sheet. If your latest action was in the Character Sheet, you can also use CTRL+Z and CTRL+Y.
Tutorial.menuEditDrawer_content: These two buttons let you enable Edit Mode or open your Drawer. We'll get back to them in a bit.
Tutorial.saveCharacter_content: This button lets you save your current Character as a file inside your Drawer, more on that later.
Tutorial.exportCharacter_content: This button lets you export your current Character, prompting your browser to download a file.
Tutorial.importCharacter_content: This button prompts you to choose a file. If you give it a Character file from Characters of the Mist, it will load that file into the app as your new active Character.
Tutorial.importComponent_content: This button also prompts you to choose a file. If you give it a Card or Tracker file from Characters of the Mist, it will load it and add it to your current Character.
Tutorial.resetCharacter_content: This button completely deletes your active Character, turning your Character Sheet into a fresh blank slate to start over from.
Tutorial.openMenu_content: This button takes you back to the main menu without unloading anything. Your open tabs stay exactly as they are, so you can pick another character, start a new one, or jump right back to your sheets at any time.
Tutorial.unloadCharacter_content: This button completely unloads your active Character, deleting it and removing the entire action history for the Undo/Redo feature. This sends you back to the main menu with a clean app and no background character loaded in.
Tutorial.settings_content: This button opens up the Settings menu, where you can access features like choosing your app's color palette, light or dark mode, or reset all your data. This is also where you will find the Migration tool, which lets you migrate Legacy Characters of the Mist data to this new version.
Tutorial.appInfo_content: This button opens up an information menu containing a general "About" section for this app, licensing information, and credits.
Tutorial.patchNotes_content: This button opens up a menu where you can consult the app's patch notes history.
Tutorial.editMode_content: This button lets you enter 'Edit Mode'. This allows you to add, remove and modify cards and trackers.
Tutorial.playMode_content: When Edit Mode is enabled, the button now allows you to go back to 'Play Mode', a lightweight display of your cards and trackers. In Play Mode, only basic functionalities are available to avoid cluttering everything.
Tutorial.addStatus_content: In Edit Mode, you can now see a button to add a new Status tracker.
Tutorial.addStoryTag_content: Similarly, you can now see a button to add a new Story Tag tracker. You can evolve a Story Tag into a Story Theme with a button on the toolbar that appears when hovering over the tracker.
Tutorial.addCard_content: Furthermore, this button has now appeared in your cards list, which allows you to create a Card.
Tutorial.openAddCard_content: We're going to see how creating a Card works now.
Tutorial.creationDialog_content: This dialog allows you to define the new card's properties.
Tutorial.menuDrawer_content: This button opens the Drawer, where all your characters, themes, and items are stored. Let's go check out the Drawer.
Tutorial.drawer_content: This is your Drawer, a powerful storage system you can use to save, backup and organize individual items like Cards and Trackers, but also full Character Sheets. You can drop an external file here to import it.
Tutorial.drawerUndoRedo_content: Similarly to the side menu, the Drawer has a pair of Undo/Redo buttons. These buttons let you undo or redo actions within the Drawer. Just like with the Character Sheet, if your latest action was in the Drawer, you can use CTRL+Z and CTRL+Y.
Tutorial.drawerRichView_content: By default, the Drawer displays items with what Characters of the Mist calls a "Rich View". This is essentially a preview of the header of the card, or the full tracker. This can take a lot of space and prevent you from seeing a lot of items at once, so you can turn it on and off here.
Tutorial.drawerFolders_content: This is where your Folders live. You will find an "Add Folder" button here, which will let you create a new folder in your current location. Folders have a context menu you can access by pressing the "..." on the right. You can drag folders around with the drag handle on the left!
Tutorial.drawerItems_content: This is where your Items live. Items are Character Sheets, Cards or Trackers. You can add Items by Saving your Character, or dragging and dropping them from your Character Sheet to the Drawer. Every Item also has a context menu with several options, and can be dragged around.
Tutorial.drawerImport_content: You can import an external file into your Drawer. The file has to be a valid Characters of the Mist file, that is, a Drawer, a Character Sheet, a Card, a Folder or a Tracker.
Tutorial.drawerExport_content: This button lets you export your FULL Drawer as a file.
Tutorial.commandPalette_content: While this tutorial won't expand on it too much, Characters of the Mist offers a powerful Command Palette which lets you use the app's features with nothing more than your keyboard! To use it, simply press CTRL+K. Keep in mind this feature is oriented towards power-users.
Tutorial.closingWords_content: This is all this short tutorial has to teach you. Feel free to explore more complex features, and if you find a bug, or a problem, or need any form of help, don't hesitate to contact me on the Tools of the Mist official Discord. You can find the link in the "App info" section. Thank you so much for using Characters of the Mist!

welcome.title: Welcome to Characters of the Mist
welcome.description: Let's set up your preferences to get you started. You can always get back to this screen from the Settings

ready.title: You're All Set!

intro.title: Welcome to Characters of the Mist!
intro.description: This short tour will show you around the app and help you get your bearings.

tabs.description: You can use these tabs to switch between the different sections of the app. You can also toggle Edit/Play mode from here.

fab.description: You can open the navigation menu with this button.

menu.title: Menu
menu.description: Let's now take a look at the Menu.

sheet.title: Character Sheet
sheet.description: You can press this option to enter the Character Sheet.

trackers.title: Trackers
trackers.description: This section is where your Character Sheet's trackers live. You can find a list of Status Trackers, Story Tag Trackers and Story Theme Trackers here.

trackerSelection.title: Selecting & Reordering
trackerSelection.description: Long-press any tracker to select it. Once selected, you can use the buttons that appear to reorder your trackers up or down.

sidePanel.description: You can tap the wrench icon at the top, or swipe from your preferred side of the screen (based on your handedness setting) to open up the Toolbelt. It contains actions you can perform on your character.

fab.description: You can tap here to open the list of available actions while on the Character Sheet.

cards.title: Your Cards
cards.description: You can switch to the Theme Cards section by pressing this tab.

cardNavigation.title: Card Navigation
cardNavigation.description: This is the Theme Cards navigation bar. You can use the arrows or swipe left & right on the bar to navigate between your cards. You can also swipe up from the bar to open the card list, where you can select which card to navigate to, and reorder them.

drawer.title: Your Drawer
drawer.description: Let's now take a look at the Drawer.

drawerContent.title: Drawer Contents
drawerContent.description: Characters of the Mist lets you store and organize characters, cards and trackers here.

drawerInteractions.title: Interacting with Items
drawerInteractions.description: Tap a folder to open it. Long-press any folder or item to see more options like renaming, moving, exporting, or deleting.

drawerToolbar.title: Drawer Actions
drawerToolbar.description: You can create new folders, import items from files, or change the view mode from here.

menuActions.title: Menu Actions
menuActions.description: You can access the app's patch notes, About section, or settings from here. You can also save, export or import files, characters and items from here.

complete.title: The tour is over!
complete.description: Explore Characters of the Mist and don't hesitate to send feedback on the Tools of the Mist discord server!

MobileGestureHints.trackerLongPress: Tip: press and hold a tracker (or use its select button) to select it for the toolbelt.
MobileGestureHints.drawerLongPress: Tip: press and hold an item to drag it, or tap its ⋯ button for more options.
